pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi prescribes a five-step skill-learning method: (1) produce high volume, (2) isolate the top 10% of outputs, (3) identify the differences between the top 10% and the bottom 90%, (4) determine which difference is the causal variable, (5) eliminate the mistakes the bottom 90% keeps making and replicate the top 10%, then repeat 100 more times.

pairs.a_text: Alex Hormozi asserts that in-person conferences and events are the highest-leverage starting channel for founders raising capital from accredited investors, primarily because face-to-face interaction compresses the time required to build sufficient trust to close, compared to digital-only channels.

pairs.b_topic: Warm vs. Cold Sales Process Mismatch
pairs.a_text: Alex Hormozi asserts that when a consulting business has more inbound demand than it can convert, the real constraint is usually not lead volume but the qualification and indoctrination of prospects before a sales call — specifically, prospects arrive without knowing what the product is, making every sales call an education session instead of a close.
pairs.b_text: Alex Hormozi asserts that applying a warm-lead sales process to cold traffic is the primary cause of low close rates on outbound-generated calls.
